perturbation.original_text: Ownership of Confidential Information. The disclosing Party shall retain all right, title and interest in and to the Confidential Information, including all intellectual property represented thereby or associated therew ith, and no license or assignment, by implication, estoppel or otherwise, is granted by the disclosing Party to the receiving Party to make, have made, use, or sell any product using the Confidential Information , or a license under any patent , patent applicat ion, utility model, copyright, trade secret, trademark, service mark or any other similar industrial or intellectual property right. At no time and under no circumstances shall the receiving Party reverse -engineer , decompile or disassemble any part of the Confidential Information .
perturbation.changed_text: Ownership of Confidential Information. The disclosing Party shall retain all right, title and interest in and to the Protected Data, including all intellectual property represented thereby or associated therew ith, and no license or assignment, by implication, estoppel or otherwise, is granted by the disclosing Party to the receiving Party to make, have made, use, or sell any product using the Protected Data , or a license under any patent , patent applicat ion, utility model, copyright, trade secret, trademark, service mark or any other similar industrial or intellectual property right. At no time and under no circumstances shall the receiving Party reverse -engineer , decompile or disassemble any part of the Protected Data .
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Information" to "Protected Data" in Section 3 without defining "Protected Data" creates ambiguity. It's unclear if Protected Data and Confidential Information are the same. This creates a contradiction with the prior section, that defines what is 'Confidential Information', and Section 1, which refers to "Confidential Information" as defined in Section 2, leading to confusion about ownership and restrictions on use. Is the ownership referring to what was mentioned in section 2? or something entirely different, since it isn't defined what "Protected Data" is. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition of Confidential Information. The term "Confiden tial Information" shall mean for purposes of this Agreement, only the financial statements explicitly marked as 'Confidential' and provided after the execution of this Agreement by a transmitting Pa rty to the receiving Party, including, without limitation, financial statements.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information,' which is defined elsewhere in the contract. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data' without defining 'Protected Data.' This creates ambiguity and contradicts the section that defines 'Confidential Information,' as it's unclear whether the provisions related to 'Protected Data' are subject to the same definition. This creates a strong in-text contradiction according to the Misaligned Terminology definition.

perturbation.original_text: Responsibility for Breach. Each Party agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any third party to whom such Party has provided Confidential Information, or any portion thereof. If a Party or any third party to whom such Party has provided Confidential Information becomes legally compelled (by oral question, deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process or by rule, regulation or other applicable law) to disclose any Confidential Information, such Party shall promptly notify the other Party of such requirement before any disclosure is made so that the other Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy or may waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement. If such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or if a Party waives compliance with the provisions hereof, each Party agrees that only that portion of the Confidential Information which it is legally required to disclose (as advised by a written opinion of counsel) will be disclosed, and each Party agrees to exercise its best efforts to obtain assurance that the Confidential Information will be treated confidentially upon disclosur e.
perturbation.changed_text: Responsibility for Breach. Each Participant agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any third party to whom such Participant has provided Confidential Information, or any portion thereof. If a Participant or any third party to whom such Participant has provided Confidential Information becomes legally compelled (by oral question, deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process or by rule, regulation or other applicable law) to disclose any Confidential Information, such Participant shall promptly notify the other Participant of such requirement before any disclosure is made so that the other Participant may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy or may waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement. If such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or if a Participant waives compliance with the provisions hereof, each Party agrees that only that portion of the Confidential Information which it is legally required to disclose (as advised by a written opinion of counsel) will be disclosed, and each Participant agrees to exercise its best efforts to obtain assurance that the Confidential Information will be treated confidentially upon disclosur e.
perturbation.explanation: In section 4, there is an inconsistency created by using "Participant" and "Party" interchangeably in the same paragraph. This raises uncertainty about whether obligations and rights described apply to both 'Participants' and 'Parties' or exclusively to one, creating an in-text contradiction. Specifically, it is not clear whether or not 'each Party agrees that only that portion of the Confidential Information which it is legally required to disclose' applies to the other Participants too. This directly affects how the agreement is interpreted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Responsibility for Breach. Each Participant agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any third party to whom such Participant has provided Confidential Information, or any portion thereof. If a Participant or any third party to whom such Participant has provided Confidential Information becomes legally compelled (by oral question, deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process or by rule, regulation or other applicable law) to disclose any Confidential Information, such Participant shall promptly notify the other Participant of such requirement before any disclosure is made so that the other Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy or may waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement. If such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or if a Participant waives compliance with the provisions hereof, each Party agrees that only that portion of the Confidential Information which it is legally required to disclose (as advised by a written opinion of counsel) will be disclosed, and each Participant agrees to exercise its best efforts to obtain assurance that the Confidential Information will be treated confidentially upon disclosur e.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Party' to 'Participant' in the Changed Text, while maintaining both terms inconsistently in the same section, creates a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction. The original text consistently used 'Party,' providing clarity. The modified text introduces 'Participant' and uses it interchangeably with 'Party' without defining the relationship between the two, making it unclear whether obligations and rights apply to both or only one, leading to uncertainty about enforcement and compliance. Specifically, the contradicted text is located in the same section as the Changed Text. The original text did not have the contradiction, the Changed Text does introduce it. The explanation provided clearly indicates this is an in-text contradiction according to the provided definitions for Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: Form of Relief. The Parties hereby acknowledge and agree that, in the event of any breach of this Agreement by either Party, including, without limitation, the actual or threatened disclosure of a Party's Confidential Information without the express prior written consent of that Paity, such Party will suffer irreparable harm and injury and no remedy at law will afford it adequate protection against, or appropriate compensation for, such injury . Accordingly , each Party hereby agrees that, in any such event, the harmed Party shall be entitled to seek specific performance of the other Party's obligations under this Agreement , as well as such further injunctive relief as may be granted by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: Form of Relief. The Parties hereby acknowledge and agree that, in the event of any breach of this Agreement by either Party, including, without limitation, the actual or threatened disclosure of a Participant's Confidential Information without the express prior written consent of that Paity, such Party will suffer irreparable harm and injury and no remedy at law will afford it adequate protection against, or appropriate compensation for, such injury . Accordingly , each Party hereby agrees that, in any such event, the harmed Party shall be entitled to seek specific performance of the other Party's obligations under this Agreement , as well as such further injunctive relief as may be granted by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'Party' with 'Participant' in one instance in Section 6 but not in others, it becomes ambiguous whether a breach related to a Participant's Confidential Information entitles the other Party to the same legal remedies. This is because the rest of the section continues to refer to rights of "Parties". This introduces a contradiction within the same section, where some clauses seem to address 'Participants' breaches differently than 'Parties' breaches, causing uncertainty in enforcement of legal remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Form of Relief. The Parties hereby acknowledge and agree that, in the event of any breach of this Agreement by either Party, including, without limitation, the actual or threatened disclosure of a Participant's Confidential Information without the express prior written consent of that Paity, such Party will suffer irreparable harm and injury and no remedy at law will afford it adequate protection against, or appropriate compensation for, such injury . Accordingly , each Party hereby agrees that, in any such event, the harmed Party shall be entitled to seek specific performance of the other Party's obligations under this Agreement , as well as such further injunctive relief as may be granted by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Party' with 'Participant' in one instance within the Form of Relief section, while the rest of the section, including the contradicted text (which is actually the same section), continues to refer to the rights and obligations of 'Parties'. This creates a contradiction, as it becomes unclear whether a breach involving a 'Participant's Confidential Information' triggers the same remedies as a breach by a 'Party', causing ambiguity and uncertainty in the enforcement of legal remedies. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the terms are used inconsistently without a clear definition, leading to conflicting obligations.
